categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue/EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividends, capital returns, and any article reporting specific financial results versus expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data mentions, index movements, and multi-stock previews where META is mentioned alongside peers rather than as the primary subject.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and institutional positioning commentary on META.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Infrastructure
categories.description: Development and release of AI models (Llama, Movie Gen, Meta AI assistant), AI infrastructure investments, custom chips, data center buildouts, open-source AI strategy, and AI-powered product features.

categories.label: Regulatory & Legal Actions
categories.description: Government investigations, antitrust charges, EU Digital Markets Act and Digital Services Act proceedings, privacy fines, court rulings, lawsuits from states or regulators, and compliance disputes across jurisdictions.

categories.label: Youth Safety & Content Moderation
categories.description: Teen account protections, child safety lawsuits, content moderation policies, sextortion enforcement, Senate hearings on child exploitation, and platform integrity measures targeting minors.

categories.label: Hardware, AR/VR & Metaverse
categories.description: Quest headsets, Orion AR glasses, Reality Labs developments, EssilorLuxottica smart eyewear partnership, mixed reality ecosystem, and related hardware strategy.

categories.label: Privacy & Data Practices
categories.description: Data collection and consent disputes, GDPR and PIPEDA enforcement, pay-or-consent advertising model challenges, facial recognition settlements, EU data use restrictions, and privacy policy controversies.

categories.label: Global Platform Operations
categories.description: Country-specific bans and access restrictions (Turkey, Russia), product launches by region (Threads in Europe, India expansions), government content removal requests, platform outages, and geopolitical content disputes.

dimensions.scale: 1=routine operational update, minor product tweak, or filler mention with no direct impact on revenue or margins, 10=event directly and substantially affects META's revenue, margins, total addressable market, or long-term strategic position
1=outcome or content fully expected, in line with prior guidance and consensus, 10=outcome or revelation completely unexpected, contradicting prior assumptions or consensus expectations
1=affects only the next few days with no lasting consequence, 10=structural shift with implications for years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive or negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone conveying urgency or significance
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no concrete data, 10=hard quantitative data with specific figures, percentages, or financial metrics
1=ambiguous implications that could be interpreted as either positive or negative for META, 10=unambiguously good or bad for META with clear directional impact
1=affects only META specifically, 10=macro or market-wide event affecting many sectors and companies
1=no regulatory, legal, or governmental dimension, 10=major regulatory action, court ruling, or legislative change with direct enforcement consequences for META
1=no effect on META's competitive position relative to peers, 10=major shift in competitive landscape that materially advantages or disadvantages META versus rivals
1=no leadership, strategic, or organizational dimension, 10=major strategic pivot, C-suite action, or organizational restructuring signaling a new direction
1=reinforces the prevailing investor narrative about META, 10=fundamentally challenges or changes the dominant investment thesis
1=rehash or follow-up of a widely known story with no new information, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=transient story that resolves within a day and leaves no residue, 10=persistent story that remains relevant and discussed for weeks or longer

kept.materiality: Ranges from routine blog posts about new Instagram stickers to $1.4B legal settlements and quarterly earnings that moved the stock 16-20%. Wide variation across the corpus.
kept.surprise: Ranges from scheduled product announcements at Connect conference to the Q1 2024 earnings guidance shock that triggered a 16% drop, and the first-ever dividend declaration. Clear variation in how unexpected outcomes were.
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from brief platform outages lasting hours to structural shifts like EU DMA non-compliance proceedings and the open-source AI strategy that will shape META for years.
kept.sentiment_strength: Ranges from neutral Form 4 filings and routine blog posts to extremely negative articles about child exploitation hearings and extremely positive articles about tripled EPS and first dividend.
kept.information_density: Ranges from vague sector roundup mentions and opinion pieces to earnings releases with specific EPS, revenue, margin figures, and settlement amounts.
kept.directional_clarity: Ranges from ambiguous articles like EU AI regulation (unclear net impact) to unambiguously positive dividend initiation or unambiguously negative $1.4B settlement payment.
kept.scope: Ranges from META-only events like Orion AR glasses launch to market-wide macro articles about Fed policy, sector rotation, and Supreme Court social media rulings affecting all platforms.
kept.regulatory_risk: High variation from zero-regulatory product launches to EU antitrust charges, DMA non-compliance proceedings, $91M GDPR fines, and state attorney general lawsuits. Central theme in this corpus.
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from no competitive angle in routine content moderation posts to Llama 3 release challenging OpenAI, EssilorLuxottica deal reshaping AR hardware competition, and TikTok ban potentially redirecting advertisers to META.
kept.management_signal: Ranges from no leadership angle in regulatory roundups to Zuckerberg's Senate apology, AI team reorganizations, Reality Labs layoffs, and strategic decisions like breaking up the Responsible AI team.
kept.narrative_shift: Ranges from articles reinforcing the efficiency/AI growth narrative to the Q1 2024 guidance shock that temporarily reversed the 'Year of Efficiency' thesis and raised fears of another spending spree.
kept.repeatedness: Wide variation from repeated EU Big Tech crackdown roundup articles that appear 10+ times with near-identical content to genuinely novel first reports like the Texas facial recognition settlement or Orion glasses reveal.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Essential for earnings articles. Ranges from dividend declarations matching expectations to Q4 2023 results where EPS tripled and Q1 2024 where guidance disappointed despite a revenue beat. Most non-earnings articles score 1.
kept.expected_duration: Ranges from platform outages resolving in hours to EU regulatory proceedings and privacy framework disputes that persist for months or years.

dropped.actionability: Most META articles are either clearly actionable (earnings, upgrades) or clearly not (blog posts, policy updates). The variation maps almost perfectly onto materiality and information_density, making this redundant.
dropped.controversy: While some articles touch divisive topics (Gaza content, political speech), the investor-relevant controversy is low and inconsistent. Most regulatory and legal articles have clear directional implications rather than genuine investor division.
